question: How to add meta tags for SEO in HTML
description: Essential <meta> and <link> tags every page needs: charset, viewport, title, description, canonical, Open Graph, and Twitter Card.

steps.title: Fundamentals — charset, viewport, title
steps.explanation: charset must be the very first tag inside <head>. The viewport meta tag prevents mobile browsers from zooming out. Title is the most important on-page SEO signal.
steps.code:
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Page Title — Brand Name</title>
  <meta name="description" content="A clear, 150–160 character description of this page that entices clicks in search results." />
</head>

steps.title: Canonical URL and robots
steps.explanation: The canonical link tells search engines which URL is the definitive version of the page, preventing duplicate-content penalties. robots controls indexing and link-following.
steps.code:
<!-- Tell Google this is the authoritative URL -->
<link rel="canonical" href="https://example.com/blog/my-post" />

<!-- Index the page and follow its links (default) -->
<meta name="robots" content="index, follow" />

<!-- Block indexing of thin/filtered pages -->
<meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow" />

steps.title: Open Graph tags for social sharing
steps.explanation: Open Graph tags control how your page looks when shared on Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack, and most other platforms. og:title, og:description, og:image, and og:url are the minimum.
steps.code:
<meta property="og:type" content="article" />
<meta property="og:url" content="https://example.com/blog/my-post" />
<meta property="og:title" content="My Post Title" />
<meta property="og:description" content="A compelling preview description, ideally under 150 characters." />
<meta property="og:image" content="https://example.com/blog/my-post-og.png" />
<meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" />
<meta property="og:image:height" content="630" />
<meta property="og:site_name" content="My Site" />

steps.title: Twitter Card tags
steps.explanation: Twitter reads its own subset of meta tags. summary_large_image displays a large preview image in tweets. If open graph tags are already set, Twitter falls back to them for title/description.
steps.code:
<meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
<meta name="twitter:site" content="@YourHandle" />
<meta name="twitter:title" content="My Post Title" />
<meta name="twitter:description" content="A short description for the tweet card preview." />
<meta name="twitter:image" content="https://example.com/blog/my-post-twitter.png" />

full_example.code:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <!-- Required -->
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>How to Bake Sourdough Bread — Baker's Guide</title>
  <meta name="description" content="Step-by-step sourdough bread recipe for beginners. Learn to make a crispy, open-crumb loaf at home with just flour, water, salt, and a starter." />

  <!-- Canonical -->
  <link rel="canonical" href="https://bakerguide.com/sourdough" />
  <meta name="robots" content="index, follow" />

  <!-- Open Graph -->
  <meta property="og:type" content="article" />
  <meta property="og:url" content="https://bakerguide.com/sourdough" />
  <meta property="og:title" content="How to Bake Sourdough Bread" />
  <meta property="og:description" content="A beginner-friendly sourdough recipe with step-by-step photos." />
  <meta property="og:image" content="https://bakerguide.com/sourdough-og.jpg" />
  <meta property="og:image:width" content="1200" />
  <meta property="og:image:height" content="630" />
  <meta property="og:site_name" content="Baker's Guide" />

  <!-- Twitter Card -->
  <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" />
  <meta name="twitter:site" content="@bakersguide" />
  <meta name="twitter:image" content="https://bakerguide.com/sourdough-og.jpg" />
</head>
<body>…</body>
</html>
